title: The Sword & Sorcery Saga - On Genre Semantics
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ROV6ce9dkfI

Daniel (@dungeondive) tackles the thorny issue of genre definitions in this short video addressing semantic debates within the sword & sorcery community. After researching various definitions from experts like L. Sprague de Camp, Lin Carter, and contemporary authors, he discovers that even the genre's authorities can't agree on precise boundaries. Some experts use heroic fantasy and sword & sorcery interchangeably, while others maintain strict separations that would narrow the genre to almost nothing.

Rather than renaming his series to be more inclusive, Daniel decides to embrace definitional flexibility, taking a more pragmatic approach guided by respected anthologies. He resolves to be inclusive with his selections while justifying any contentious inclusions through reference to the major voices that shaped the genre. The video emphasizes that genre definitions exist as useful maps for discussion rather than rigid classifications.

----

How do you typically define sword & sorcery when recommending books to others—do you use strict criteria or a more intuitive sense?
